sql: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS failed_transactions_new(id_failed_transaction INTEGER, co_failed_transaction VARCHAR(30) PRIMARY KEY, co_transaction VARCHAR(40), type VARCHAR(30), error_code VARCHAR(10), error_message VARCHAR(255), transaction_object TEXT, da_failed TIMESTAMP, co_user TEXT NOT NULL, id_user INTEGER NOT NULL, co_enterprise TEXT NOT NULL, id_enterprise INTEGER NOT NULL);
description: Tabla intermedia con nuevo esquema de failed_transactions (PK co_failed_transaction, columnas usuario/empresa).

sql: INSERT INTO failed_transactions_new(id_failed_transaction, co_failed_transaction, co_transaction, type, error_code, error_message, transaction_object, da_failed, co_user, id_user, co_enterprise, id_enterprise) SELECT id_failed_transaction, strftime('%s','now') || '.' || id_failed_transaction, co_transaction, type, error_code, error_message, transaction_object, da_failed, '', 0, '', 0 FROM failed_transactions;
description: Migra filas legadas conservando id_failed_transaction y generando co_failed_transaction.
Elimina tabla antigua con PK autoincrement.

sql: ALTER TABLE failed_transactions_new RENAME TO failed_transactions;
